title: tilia caroliniana subsp. heterophylla
intro: Tilia caroliniana subsp. heterophylla, also known as American Linden, is a deciduous tree native to the southeastern United States, characterized by its broad, heart-shaped leaves and fragrant flowers. The medicinal properties of American Linden are attributed to its leaves, which contain salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, and have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of American Linden include its ability to reduce fever, soothe respiratory issues, and alleviate pain, making it a popular herbal remedy for colds, flu, and headaches. American Linden can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to promote relaxation, reduce stress, and improve overall well-being.
